blocks.content: The Trap of Choosing Between Two Choices (2)

blocks.speaker: Orkamph
blocks.content: Shirone, even something beautiful is just a monstrosity when seen up close. The world is not beautiful to anyone but you, and showing emotions is never good. Especially if you want to protect the people you love.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Now was the time to devise a solution rather than get angry.

blocks.speaker: Orkamph
blocks.content: What should I do from now on?

blocks.speaker: Orkamph
blocks.content: Show them your strength. If they want a clear reason to choose sides, providing them with solid evidence could be one approach.
Orkamph finally revealed what he had truly wanted to say.

blocks.speaker: Orkamph
blocks.content: I'll arrange a place. Give a demonstration of Ataraxia. If you show them your power there, at least you'll force them to distinguish friend from foe. Then, at the very least, they won't touch your family.
Shirone's gaze lowered.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: As his mind settled calmly, a hot sensation came up his throat and got stuck in his throat.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: So that's... the real reason you called me here.
Orkamph let out a small breath.
As expected, lies didn't work on Shirone.

blocks.speaker: Orkamph
blocks.content: Have you heard of the Valkyrie? It is an army organized by Terraje in preparation for the Final War. The reason Kazra Kingdom was able to survive economically was that we secured contracts for weapons from them. But the situation isn't optimistic. The reason Terraje planted their children here is to eventually drive me out and swallow Kazra one day. We need to strengthen our influence within Valkyrie even more. Your Ataraxia is fully capable of doing so.
Shirone felt the strength drain from his body.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: He had vaguely guessed, but tears welled up in his eyes when he heard these words from someone he thought was his birth parent.

blocks.speaker: Orkamph
blocks.content: I'm asking you. Show them. Just by demonstrating it before the nobles, the impact will be immense. It's an opportunity to sway the neutral faction to our side.
Shirone forced himself to swallow the tears that were threatening to spill.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Did you think I would obey readily if you said that?

blocks.speaker: Orkamph
blocks.content: On the contrary. That's why I'm thinking of making a proposal. You said before that you wanted to go home. Does that still hold true?

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: No, it's not. I want to go back even more than before.
Orkamph wasn't displeased by Shirone's sarcastic words.

blocks.speaker: Orkamph
blocks.content: If you perform the demonstration, I'll let you return home as soon as the paternity test is finished. You can go to the magic academy or do whatever you wish. You won't even have to come to the royal palace if you don't want to. What do you think?

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Now that he had experienced the royal palace, he realized that returning home wouldn't be as simple as he initially thought.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: But then Ataraxia won't be available for you. I'll be needed eventually, so how do you plan on convincing the nobles?
Orkamph felt uneasy as if his true intentions had been exposed.
The reason he proposed the demonstration was for a much greater purpose than drawing in the neutrals.

blocks.speaker: Orkamph
blocks.content: Arius had said that if he saw Ataraxia directly, he could find a way to replicate it.

blocks.speaker: Orkamph
blocks.content: Once replication succeeds, Shirone would be unnecessary. No, rather, it would be better for him to leave the royal palace. Even for Shirone's own life...
That's why a secret agreement is necessary.

blocks.speaker: Orkamph
blocks.content: I'll announce to the nobles that you will return after graduating from the magic academy. Would one year suffice? During that time, I will purge Terraje Faction. After that, we can each go our own way.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: However, Shirone nodded as if that level of risk was natural.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: He was planning to move his residence once he graduated from the magic academy anyway.
It's just a matter of thinking that the moving distance has become a bit farther.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: At least until he had gained enough power to stand unshaken by royal authority, he could live that way.

blocks.speaker: Orkamph
blocks.content: Who will be attending the demonstration?

blocks.speaker: Orkamph
blocks.content: I've already sent word. All the high-ranking nobles will gather. They're expecting a grand show.
Shirone looked at Orkamph with sorrowful eyes.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: You knew from the beginning that I couldn't refuse.

blocks.speaker: Orkamph
blocks.content: Shirone, it was you who first requested this proposal three days ago. I merely created a justification to allow it.
Orkamph remained cold to the end.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: But thanks to that, Shirone regained his composure.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Fine. Let me know when the preparations are finished. For now, I need to go see my 'parents.'
He wanted to just leave right away.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: He also knew that even if he did so, Orkamph wouldn't reproach him.
But Shirone maintained his composure and exited with courtesy.
After all, Orkamph was the king of a neighboring kingdom.
Upon arriving at the quarters, Shirone first checked on his parents' well-being.
Their eyes were sunken as if they hadn't slept all night.
Though they were forcing smiles so as not to worry their child, Olena's face was as pale as a blank sheet.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Even for Shirone, who had trained in magic, his heart was racing.
For her mother, who had lived her life without causing harm to others, the stress must have been unbearable.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: 'I'm sorry, Mother.'
Amy, who greeted him, neither forced a bright smile nor showed signs of tension.
She just raised her hand in greeting with a bitter smile.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Shirone felt a strong sense of camaraderie in her appearance.
Reina, who had been in a meeting with the attendants in the next room, greeted him with a bright expression.

blocks.speaker: Reina
blocks.content: You're here, Shirone. We've been waiting.
Despite everyone looking worn out, Reina alone seemed full of energy.
As expected of a court musician, she was clearly accustomed to the palace atmosphere.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: While it was refreshing to see this new side of her, Shirone couldn't shake the discomfort in that moment.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: I met Orkamph.
Reina's expression hardened.
Not only from Shirone's cold voice, but also from the way he addressed the king by name, one could guess how angry he was about this incident.

blocks.speaker: Reina
blocks.content: I'm sorry for not telling you in advance. But I was afraid you'd worry...

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: I understand the reason. But everyone was in danger.
Reina swallowed hard.
It was the expression that appeared only when Shirone was truly angry, which she had seen once at their family estate.

blocks.speaker: Reina
blocks.content: It's okay now. We've strengthened the security procedures. We've requested the guard to further enhance security...

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: You should have told me.
Reina's mouth shut tight like a clam.
Amy, watching the situation unfold as if she were observing a fire, let out a sigh.

blocks.speaker: Amy
blocks.content: 'See? I told you we should have said something.'
This mess was a result of underestimating how much Shirone cared about his family.

blocks.speaker: Amy
blocks.content: But honestly, Amy was in no position to say anything either.
Even though she had clearly anticipated Shirone's reaction, she had followed Reina's judgment because she knew Reina's decision was ultimately the right one.

blocks.speaker: Amy
blocks.content: Even now, with Shirone fuming like this, if he had found out earlier, the situation could have spiralled out of control.

blocks.speaker: Amy
blocks.content: There's no way Shirone wouldn't have known this fact.
That's why, in the end, it was Reina's victory.
Vincent, feeling the awkwardness, tried to console Shirone.

blocks.speaker: Vincent
blocks.content: Shirone, Ms. Reina had no choice. Moreover, it was a decision made after seeking our consent. If you're upset, be mad at me.
Shirone lowered his head.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: He had known that from the beginning.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: I'm sorry. I know Reina Noona saved all of us. I just... I felt so frustrated, and it got the better of me.
Reina heard the faint tremor in Shirone's voice.

blocks.speaker: Reina
blocks.content: What on earth did Orkamph say to him that this usually strong child returned so broken?

blocks.speaker: Reina
blocks.content: No, I'm the one who should apologize. You had every right to be upset. Informing you was also one of my duties. From now on, I'll report everything without omission.
As Shirone managed a forced smile, Reina finally relaxed a bit.

blocks.speaker: Reina
blocks.content: As expected, this child was the most beautiful when he was smiling.
It made Reina even more determined not to see him sad.

blocks.speaker: Amy
blocks.content: So what did you talk about when you went to the audience chamber?

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Ah, that's….

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Shirone said what Orkamph had proposed.
Amy sank into careful thought, then nodded.

blocks.speaker: Amy
blocks.content: Hmm, it's a decent proposal. Of course, assuming he keeps his promise.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: It doesn't matter. We have to move after graduation anyway.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: We can think about the next problem when we get there. So I accepted the proposal for now.

blocks.speaker: Amy
blocks.content: That makes sense. Same for me.
The concept of a 'main family' for nobles exists because their place of residence changes depending on their profession.

Amy's older brother, Diane, was the head of the archers' division at the royal palace, and her second brother, Ares, who was an explorer, was always wandering remote places, so his current whereabouts were unknown.
Globally, the employment rate for mages is about 80 percent, and the hiring rate is close to 99 percent.
The reason the employment rate is lower than the hiring rate is that there are quite a few mages who refuse jobs.
But competition was fierce, and most students, upon graduating, moved to the capital, Bashka, to find work or prepare for certification exams.
If those options don't pan out, many went abroad for further studies.
Although there are magic institutions in Creas too, Shirone didn't want to end his magic career in a provincial region.

In the future, when he reached the certified fifth class, he might be appointed as the vice-chairman of the Magic Association Creas Branch, but right now, there were many things he wanted to accomplish.
Alpheas Magic Academy was a kingdom-designated institution, and upon graduation, you receive a non-official tenth rank mage certificate.
Once you obtain the certificate, guilds give you work, so you can also live as a mercenary.
This way, he wouldn't even need to buy a house, making it more convenient to move around.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: He thought that maybe, after living like that for a while, he might meet someone special and eventually settle down somewhere.
Amy, as if supporting Shirone's resolve, clapped her hands and cheered him on.

blocks.speaker: Amy
blocks.content: Alright! If we're going to do this, let's do it right!
The Colosseum was built in a basin area 2 kilometers north of Kazra Royal Palace.
It used to be a huge gambling den where warriors fought wild beasts and nobles placed bets.
However, due to financial issues in the royal palace, it had been indefinitely closed.
The unkempt sandy ground still bore traces of blood.
No matter how much the workers swept with their brooms, the red bloodstains wouldn't be erased.
The nobles entered with their guards, and Amy and Reina also took their seats in the third-floor stands.
Orkamph had considered Shirone's feelings and allowed his parents to attend, but since Olena's health had worsened, it was decided that Vincent would stay behind at the quarter with her.
Shirone was waiting for his time in the waiting room underground the Colosseum.
Around him, there were exercise machines and sandbags once used by champions, and various weapons were displayed on shelves.
As the iron door of the waiting room opened, Zion and Uorin entered.
It seemed to be Uorin's first time in the underground waiting room, as she curiously glanced around.
Shirone no longer welcomed anyone.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: After learning of last night's events, everyone seemed like an enemy to him.

blocks.speaker: Zion
blocks.content: What a desolate place. I wonder what thoughts went through the minds of the warriors waiting here.

blocks.speaker: Zion
blocks.content: Were they praying not to be eaten by the lions? Or perhaps they were planning how to break down that door and escape.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: No, they must have been determined to kill their enemy.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Because that's the only path left for a warrior.
Zion smiled crookedly.
As expected, when it came to words, he was no match for a mage.
